Title: Source-sink synergy is the key unlocking sweet potato starch yield potential

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases storage-root starch and sucrose contents and promotes source-sink synergy; RNAi decreases them; fresh yield not significantly changed
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate for food and industrial starch production
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in storage roots; increases from 60 to 90 DAP; induced by 175 mM sucrose in leaves, peaking at 6 h
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic sweet potato, function evident in leaves and storage roots; endogenous IbPMA1 highly expressed in storage roots/phloem-related tissues
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 175 mM sucrose treatment; field growth; source-sink conditions during 60-130 DAP
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Haplotype-based association analysis in F1 population; overexpression; RNAi knockdown; subcellular localization; RT-qPCR; 13CO2 stable isotope labeling; metabolite profiling; histological starch staining
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: IbSUT1; IbSUT2; IbSUT3; IbSUT4; IbSWEET1; IbSWEET2; IbSWEET4; IbSWEET12; IbSWEET15; IbCWIN; IbSTP12; IbbHLH49
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant difference in fresh yield or cell density reported in transgenic lines
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics, ProteomeXchange PXD048050; RNA-seq and resequencing, NGDC PRJCA022251
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbPMA1 haplotypes are significantly associated with dry matter/starch accumulation but not fresh yield in the F1 population. 2. IbPMA1 encodes a plasma membrane H+-ATPase localized to the cell membrane and is highly expressed in storage roots. 3. IbPMA1 overexpression increases photosynthetic capacity, sucrose transport-related gene expression, 13C allocation to storage roots, and starch accumulation, while RNAi causes the opposite effect. 4. IbPMA1 acts as a key source-sink synergy factor promoting starch yield formation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for high-starch breeding and engineering; linked simplex SNPs/haplotypes may serve as markers for selecting high dry matter/high starch genotypes; can be combined with regulators enhancing source-sink flux
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it is a proton pump/transport facilitator rather than a biosynthetic enzyme or TF; six haplotypes identified, with homozygous linked SNP state associated with higher dry matter.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ADP-glucose formation step in starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as part of the first rate-limiting enzyme AGPase in starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of ADP-glucose from glucose-1-phosphate and ATP as part of AGPase
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate activated glucosyl donor precursor for starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate for food and industrial starch production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated by IbbHLH49 and associated with enhanced starch biosynthesis and starch accumulation in storage roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in H283 than L423 storage roots; direct target of IbbHLH49 in leaves and storage roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in IbbHLH49-OE lines and downregulated in IbbHLH49-RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid/amyloplast (strongly inferable for AGPase in starch biosynthesis)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteome analysis; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC; ChIP-qPCR; RT-qPCR; enzyme activity assay of AGPase in IbbHLH49 transgenics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbAGPS is more abundant/highly expressed in the high-starch genotype H283. 2. It is directly bound and transcriptionally activated by IbbHLH49. 3. Because AGPase is the first rate-limiting enzyme in starch biosynthesis, activation of IbAGPS contributes to increased starch accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme target for increasing starch biosynthesis; especially valuable in stacking with IbbHLH49 and source-sink transport genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses AGPS as crucial because only IbbHLH49 among tested TFs directly targeted its promoter.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces alpha-1,6 branch points into glucan chains during starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate for food and industry
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated by IbbHLH49 and associated with enhanced starch biosynthesis and altered starch composition
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in H283 than L423 storage roots; co-expressed with starch accumulation-related genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in IbbHLH49-OE lines and downregulated in IbbHLH49-RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteome analysis; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC; ChIP-qPCR; RT-qPCR; SBE activity assay in IbbHLH49 transgenics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbSBEI is a starch biosynthetic enzyme with higher abundance in the high-starch genotype H283. 2. Multiple TFs can bind its promoter, and IbbHLH49 directly activates it. 3. Increased SBE activity in IbbHLH49-OE plants supports a causal role in enhanced starch accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for stacking with AGPase and source-sink regulators to improve starch content and optimize starch structure
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter is a convergence point for several candidate TFs identified in the paper.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor; clade XII; conserved bHLH domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Photosynthate production, loading, unloading, and starch biosynthesis; source-sink synergy
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: IbTPT; IbPMA1; IbSUT2; IbSUT4; IbAGPS; IbSBEI
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates IbTPT, IbPMA1, IbSUT2, IbSUT4, IbAGPS, and IbSBEI transcription by binding their promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Pivotal regulator coordinating source and sink processes to improve both fresh yield and starch accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Sucrose-inducible positive regulation of source-sink/starch genes; explicit loop not established
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate for food and industrial uses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases photosynthetic rate, fresh yield, starch content, AGPase and SBE activities, and 13C allocation to storage roots; RNAi has opposite effects
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression peaks at 70 and 100 DAP in storage roots; highest in stems of 3-month-old plants; strongly induced by 175 mM sucrose in leaves, peaking at 3 h
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic sweet potato, active in leaves and storage roots where it upregulates source-sink and starch genes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 175 mM sucrose treatment; developmental stages; field growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H screening; EMSA; dual-LUC; overexpression; RNAi; ChIP-qPCR; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; photosynthesis assays; 13CO2 labeling; enzyme activity assays
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: IbTPT; IbPMA1; IbSUT2; IbSUT4; IbAGPS; IbSBEI
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No negative tradeoff reported; overexpression improved fresh yield and starch accumulation simultaneously
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq PRJCA022251; co-expression analysis with transcriptome data; proteomics context PXD048050
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbbHLH49 was identified from an IbPMA1-promoter Y1H screen as a key candidate regulator. 2. It directly binds and activates promoters of IbPMA1, IbAGPS, and IbSBEI, and in transgenic plants also IbTPT, IbSUT2, and IbSUT4. 3. IbbHLH49 overexpression enhances source activity in leaves, sink unloading and starch biosynthesis in storage roots, thereby increasing both fresh yield and starch content. 4. RNAi lines show opposite effects, confirming causality.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for simultaneously improving storage-root yield and starch content; suitable for transgenic or editing-based activation and for stacking with IbPMA1/AGPS/SBEI alleles
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among the nine TFs identified, IbbHLH49 uniquely targeted IbAGPS in addition to IbPMA1 and IbSBEI, supporting its central role.
